teamObjective.explanation: Deterministically repaired ToM trace with objective protect_partner.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner P3 has 3 cards and has not yet responded. Since P0 is near finish, P3 likely wants our team to win this trick to deny P0 initiative, but may not have a heart single that can beat 3 cheaply. P0's lead of a low heart may also signal that P0 is trying to empty their hand, so P3 would prefer I take control if I can do so cheaply.

opponentBelief.summary: P0 (opponent) has only 2 cards left and led a low heart single, probably trying to shed a weak card while keeping a stronger card for a later winning play or finish. If P0 regains initiative, they may go out soon. P2 may hold higher hearts or trumps to contest if I pass.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Selected play-single-heart-4-copy1 after deterministic schema repair of ToM reasoning.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Passing conserves all my cards but cedes the trick to opponents. With P0 at 2 cards, losing initiative here is risky because P0 may finish soon or set up a strong lead.
Beating with the 9 wins the trick but spends a higher card unnecessarily when the 4 suffices, leaving me weaker in future heart-singles.
Beating with the 10 is even more wasteful; the 10 is the strongest card I have and should be reserved for a trick where a higher card is required.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.

notes: Deterministically schema-repaired from a ToM-prompted raw output; selectedActionId is unchanged.
